zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Fitzsimon of Ormsby於大約1315年出生時，她的父親Ralph fitzSimon of Ormesby42歲，母親Margaret Fitzralph38歲。她於1315年與William Skipwith在英國越可爾福藍波洛結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。她去世於1392年在英國越可爾斯吉維茲享年78歲。

nameMeanings.name: Fitzsimon
meanings: Irish: Mac, son, and Shimoin, Simon, meaning son of Simon

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Fitzsimon of Ormsby was born about 1315, her father, Ralph fitzSimon of Ormesby, was 42 and her mother, Margaret Fitzralph, was 38. She married William Skipwith in 1315, in Flamborough, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. She died in 1392, in Skipwith, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 78.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Fitzsimon of Ormsby est née environ 1315, son père, Ralph fitzSimon of Ormesby, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Margaret Fitzralph, avait 38 ans. Elle a épousé William Skipwith en 1315, à Flamborough, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Elle est décédée en 1392, à Skipwith, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 78 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Fitzsimon of Ormsby(이)가 대략 1315년에 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Ralph fitzSimon of Ormesby(은)는 42세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Fitzralph(은)는 38세였다. 그녀는 1315년 영국 요르크실르 플램루그h에서 William Skipwith(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1392년 영국 요르크실르 스키p위스에서 78세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Fitzsimon of Ormsby родилась около 1315 г., её отцу по имени Ralph fitzSimon of Ormesby было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Fitzralph, 38. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Skipwith в 1315 г. в местности флэмбороую, еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Она умерла в 1392 г. в местности скипвит, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 78 лет.
